channel.title: Thanissaro Bhikkhu Quotes
channel.description: Dhamma quotes by Thanissaro Bhikkhu, updated every 6 hours

item.title: The third Guardian meditation: the foulness of the body. You can think about that in a consoling way or a more stringent way. The consoling…
item.description:
Thanissaro Bhikkhu wrote:
The third Guardian meditation: the foulness of the body. You can think about that in a consoling way or a more stringent way. The consoling way is to remind yourself that many of the heavy issues in life are based on meeting the needs of the body. But look at the body. What is it? Just a few organs that are going to function together for a while and then fall apart. And a lot of these heavy issues around the body are really not all that important. Why make the body such a big deal?

As for the stringent side, when you see that you’re really attached to the body, ask yourself: Well, what is it here in the body that’s really worth being proud about? The Buddha once said, after cataloging all the different things that the body does, all the stuff inside the body, and then what happens to the body as it ages and dies, “Whoever would think of exalting himself or disparaging others on the basis of this body: What is that if not blindness?” In other words, your body may be stronger, more fit, more beautiful, whatever, but so what? It’s still made of disgusting things and oozes disgusting things, and is subject to all sorts of diseases. It dies. So what’s so great about that?
From: People Suffer from Their Thinking by Thanissaro Bhikkhu
